    Why Modiâs 'age of Bharat' is trumping Nehruâs 'idea of India'

    MC EXCLUSIVE Deloitte, EY, KPMG among 7 firms in race as Centre prepares nationwide audit of critical IT infrastructure

    The Modi Milestone: Building a market-driven, tech-tonic, multi-aligned India

    MARKETS
    Join Us On
    LiveTV

    • Nifty 50

      23214.95

      -27.15 (-0.12%)

    • Sensex

      73983.18

      +64.42 (+0.09%)

    Remove Ad

    Why Modiâs 'age of Bharat' is trumping Nehruâs 'idea of India'

    Modiâs new epoch has redefined India in civilisational terms, reversing the cultural project of âNehruvian Indiaâ which defined the contours of Indiaâs polity for decades after Nehruâs own term as Indiaâs first prime minister. Nalin Mehta writes on how Modiâs power play is an inverted mirror image of Nehruâs politics seventy years earlier; on the deep political, social and cultural impact of âNaMoâs Bharatâ which has irrevocably reconfigured Indiaâs polity and why the prime ministerâs redrawing of the boundaries and colours of Indiaâs palimpsest may far outlive our current politics.

    • Why Modiâs 'age of Bharat' is trumping Nehruâs 'idea of India'

    NEWS NOT TO MISS

    • Gold Rate
    • Real Estate
    • Health
    Gold & Silver ETFs Are Crashing! Here's What's Really Happening Gold and silver ETFs are under pressure as precious metal prices witness a sharp correction. Gold has fallen below $4,200 an ounce, while silver has nearly halved from its January peak. Rising US bond yields, strong jobs data, and growing expectations of Fed rate hikes are driving investors away from non-yielding assets like gold and silver. But after a massive rally over the past four years, is this just profit-booking or the start of a bigger decline? Watch to understand what's behind the crash in gold and silver ETFs and what it means for investors.
    • Silver ETFs bleed for 4th straight month, gold snaps year-long inflow streak in May

    Indian 10-year bond yield down 0.10% on tax relief-driven FPI buying Money market experts attributed the easing yields on government securities to heavy inflows of Rs 11,026.331 crore in the last four days by foreign investors in these securities under the Fully Accessible Route (FAR).

    Show More News
    Gold & Silver ETFs Are Crashing! Here's What's Really Happening Gold and silver ETFs are under pressure as precious metal prices witness a sharp correction. Gold has fallen below $4,200 an ounce, while silver has nearly halved from its January peak. Rising US bond yields, strong jobs data, and growing expectations of Fed rate hikes are driving investors away from non-yielding assets like gold and silver. But after a massive rally over the past four years, is this just profit-booking or the start of a bigger decline? Watch to understand what's behind the crash in gold and silver ETFs and what it means for investors.
    • Silver ETFs bleed for 4th straight month, gold snaps year-long inflow streak in May
    • Muthoot Finance, Manappuram, other gold financier stocks fall up to 6% as precious metal declines 2%
